avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New localised accumulations will develop in wind sheltered terrain mainly on North-East to South-East aspects. Above 900 metres where accumulations achieve depth over older snow-ice they will be moderately bonded. Gully tops and corrie rims will be most affected. Elsewhere, old snow will be firm and often icy with greatest amounts above 900 metres.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Freezing level will be around the glens overnight before lifting back up to 900 metres during the day. Snowfall during the night otherwise clear. A strong breeze from the North-North-West, veering North and easing.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild, dry day with the cloud base initially around 650 metres.  Winds were very strong from the South, easing later in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The snow cover remains well bonded and stable throughout. Greatest cover is to be found above 900 metres on the high plateau areas and on South-West through West to North aspects. Very patchy cover below this altitude. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The older snowpack will refreeze and have good stability. Localised and shallow areas of unstable windslab will persist mainly on North-East to South-East aspects above 800 metres with features such as Gully tops and corrie rims most affected. There will be some redistribution of this windslab onto North and North-East aspects during the day. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Winds become light overnight before increasing from the South-South-West during the day. It is expected to remain dry with an overnight frost followed by a cool day.
Observed Weather Influences: Strong Westerly winds. Rain gradually turned to snow through the day before becoming mainly dry later in the afternoon. The freezing level dropped to around 700 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack was starting to refreeze and has good stability. Localised and shallow areas of fresh, unstable windslab were developing mainly on North-East to South-East aspects above 800 metres, with features such as gully tops an corrie rims most affected. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Good stability in all locations. The diminished snowpack will refreeze and become hard or crusty at all elevations. Pockets of thin windslab may be in place on some steep East to South East aspects above 950m.  The avalanche hazard will be Low

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A little light snowfall very early in the period then cold and dry with light variable winds.
Observed Weather Influences: Mild and wet but with some late snow showers near summits. Moderate South West winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The diminished snowpack was moist or wet at all altitudes at the time of reporting. Stability is good in most locations. Residual cornices are wet and weak.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The older snowpack will remain well bonded and stable in all locations. Isolated deposits of shallow windslab will persist at the top of some steep North-East to South-East aspects above 1100 metres. These deposits will be confined to wind sheltered areas such and gully tops and coire rims and gradually consolidate through the day. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A cold, dry sunny day. The freezing level will be around 700 metres. Moderate Northerly winds will ease overnight becoming light Southerly by dawn and increasing in strength slightly through the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Rain at all levels overnight and through the morning  gradually turned to snow above around 750 metres and eased through the afternoon. Strong Southerly winds moderated and became North-Westerly.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack remains well bonded and stable. Isolated deposits of moderately bonded windslab are developing on some steep North-East to South-East aspects above around 1100 metres. These deposits are shallow and generally confined to some wind sheltered areas such as some gully tops and coire rims. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: New snow accumulations are not expected to be significant. The older moist snowpack will start to re-freeze and consolidate it will be well bonded, very firm and stable. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Early evening Thursday, the freezing level lowering to around 700 metres with some light new snow on fresh Westerly winds. These winds will ease becoming Southerly and then South-Westerly and dry conditions are expected. with a slightly rising freezing levels.
Observed Weather Influences: The freezing level has been above the summits for nearly 24 hours it has gradually lowered this morning to be zero degrees at 1100 metres around midday. Rain at all levels through this morning accompanied by moderate to fresh Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: A light dusting of new snow arrived in the afternoon these accumulations are not extensive and lie mainly above 950 metres.. The older moist snowpack is well bonded, very firm and stable. Largest continuous snow areas remain on North-West through North to East aspects above around 870 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised accumulations of windslab will develop above 900 metres on East to South aspects these will be isolated on South-West aspects. The greatest instability will be where accumulations gain depth in steep terrain such as gully tops, coire rims and convexities. Later in the period these accumulations will begin to consolidate. On these aspects fresh fragile cornices will develop. Elsewhere the old snowpack will be firm well bonded and stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Snowfall is expected above 400 to 500 metres on fresh North-West to North-North-East winds, this will clear to leave a dry day with light increasing to fresh Southerly winds. The 500 metre freezing level will steadily rise to summit levels by the end of the day.
Observed Weather Influences: Mostly dry through the morning before the onset of rain and drizzle through the afternoon, this turned to snowfall above 1000 metres later in the day. Winds were gale force Southerly, The freezing level at midday was around summit levels, cooler through the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack surface is thawing and is generally stable on all aspects and elevations. The greatest snow cover remains in high coires and plateau areas above 800 metres, with snow patches and ribbons extending to lower elevations. Glide cracks were observed on a steep North facing slope at around 1000 metres. Rockfall continues to be a potential hazard. The avalanche hazard is Low.
